rulings.Case Title: ELISE WILLIAMS ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Leitmotif Service Llc Dba Fluidfreeride Set Forth In Plaintiffs Deposition Notice Of Person Most Qualified
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, Feb-17-2026, LINE 1. PLAINTIFFS ELISE WILLIAMS, and ZACHARY WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents From Defendant Leitmotif Service Llc Dba Fluidfreeride Set Forth In Plaintiffs Deposition Notice Of Person Most Qualified. Plaintiffs Elise Williams et al.'s motion to compel further responses to their requests for production of documents at deposition, and for monetary and issue sanctions, against defendant Leitmotif Service LLC dba Fluidfreeride, is granted in part and denied in part. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: LI CHANG KUANG VS. KENNY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Order Denying Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, Feb-17-2026, LINE 2. DEFENDANT KENNY WONG's Motion For Reconsideration Order Denying Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. The motion for reconsideration is denied. A reconsideration motion is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 1008, subdivision (a), which states in relevant part that, "[w]hen an application for an order has been made to a judge, or to a court, and refused in whole or in part, or granted, or granted conditionally, or on terms, any party affected by the order may, within 10 days after service upon the party of written notice of entry of the order and based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law, make application to the same judge or court that made the order, to reconsider the matter and modify, amend, or revoke the prior order." Defendant Kenny Wong's motion does not present any new or different facts, circumstances, or law. Wong also seeks relief under Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 473, subdivision (b), which states that "[t]he court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or the party's legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against the party through the party's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." Wong cites surprise or mistake in his failure to contest the tentative ruling but offers no basis why the court's original decision was incorrect or based on any mistake of law or fact. The court denies sanctions to plaintiffs. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANI RODRIGUEZ VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, Feb-17-2026, LINE 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Leave To File Amended Answer. The motion for leave to file an amended answer is denied. The City's motion does not include a declaration that satisfies the requirements of Rule of Court 3.1324, subdivisions (b)(3) and (b)(4): the LaFleur Declaration does not state when facts giving rise to the amended allegations were discovered and the reasons why the request for amendment was not made earlier. The court's concern about the missing information is not merely academic. The substantive inquiry on a motion to amend a pleading largely concerns whether there is inexcusable delay or prejudice. This motion presents a close question on prejudice; there is limited time left before the close of fact discovery and the City's proposed amendment plainly presents fact and discovery issues about how the City's inspection system was substantially affected by the COVID-19 pandemic, as the City represents in its moving papers. The City's failure to disclose information about its discovery of facts underlying the need for amendment deprives Plaintiff of the ability to mount a full argument about inexcusable delay. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BENDER VS. SB TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, Feb-17-2026, LINE 11. 6-PLAINTIFF ROBERT BENDER'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT. Plaintiff's motion to strike the term "extortionate" from defendant's answer is denied. Accepting the facts in the answer as true, the use of the term plaintiff objects to is not false or improper and is not an instance of sham pleading. The court denies the request for judicial notice of the article attached as Exhibit B to the Jung Declaration because it is immaterial to the court's resolution of this motion. That a case has received publicity or that a defendant has made a comment to the press concerning allegations does not make the pleading a sham. The court disregards the facts stated in the Bender Declaration because it must accept the answer's allegations as true on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: ROBERT BENDER VS. SB TECHNOLOGY, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR for Tuesday, Feb-17-2026, LINE 11. 1-PLAINTIFF ROBERT BENDER'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Portions Of Complaint. The motion to seal portions of the complaint is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff contends that the seal is necessary to protect the privacy of nonparties. But the sealed material does not merely protect the privacy of nonparties, who are not named or described at any identifying level of detail; it also includes allegations against named defendants that form a basis for plaintiff's invocation of remedies. The court cannot find that the redactions are narrowly tailored. Defendant does not oppose sealing and raises the further issue that the allegations are inflammatory and may result in prejudice. The court does not see the same risks here of prejudice to the fairness of proceedings as were apparent in People v. Jackson (2005) 128 Cal.App.4th 1009, on which defendant relies. (See id., at p. 1025 ["The details of the crimes alleged are highly prejudicial. This, combined with the celebrity of the defendant, has created a torrent of pretrial publicity. Experience teaches that any new development in this case, real or imaginary, leads to intense media scrutiny. News sources from around the world-newspapers, magazines, radio, television and the Internet-are saturated with information (or disinformation) about the case. Unless one is a hermit, such information is hard to avoid. It is unlikely that potential jurors would not be influenced by exposure to the information NBC seeks."].) The basis for a demand for public relief in our court system is not generally private, and embarrassment alone is not generally found to override the public right of access to court documents. (Id., at p. 1024.) The court will maintain the conditional seal for 45 days to enable either party to make a narrower request for relief. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.=(301/CVA)
